# Sigil Forge

> Where agent tools are tested.

## What Forge does
- Classifies every AI agent skill and MCP server by category and capability
- Matches compatible skills + MCPs into Forge Stacks for specific use cases
- Provides Sigil trust scores for every listing (0-100 scale)
- Publishes Forge Weekly digest of new tools, trends, and security alerts
- Maintains real-time security scanning of 5,700+ skills and 2,000+ MCP servers

## API Endpoints
- Search: GET https://api.sigilsec.ai/forge/search?q={query}&type=skill|mcp|both
- Stacks: GET https://api.sigilsec.ai/forge/stack?use_case={description}
- Check: GET https://api.sigilsec.ai/forge/tools/{ecosystem}/{name}
- Classifications: GET https://api.sigilsec.ai/forge/classifications/skills
- Classifications: GET https://api.sigilsec.ai/forge/classifications/mcps

## MCP Server Tools
Install: npm install -g @nomark/sigil-mcp-server

Available tools:
- sigil_scan: Scan files/directories for security issues
- sigil_scan_package: Scan npm/pip packages before installation
- sigil_clone: Clone and scan git repositories
- sigil_check_package: Look up package risk in database
- forge_search: Search for skills and MCP servers
- forge_stack: Get curated stacks for use cases
- forge_check: Get trust score and permissions for tools

## Tool Categories
- Database Connectors: Postgres, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis integrations
- API Integrations: REST, GraphQL, webhook connections
- Code Tools: Linting, formatting, building, testing
- File/System Tools: File operations, search, directory management
- AI/LLM Tools: Prompt engineering, RAG, embeddings, chat
- Security Tools: Scanning, auditing, secret management
- DevOps Tools: Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, deployment
- Search Tools: Elasticsearch, Algolia, semantic search
- Communication Tools: Slack, Discord, email, notifications
- Data Pipeline Tools: ETL, streaming, transformation
- Testing Tools: Unit, integration, E2E testing
- Monitoring Tools: Logging, metrics, observability

## Trust Scoring
Every tool receives a trust score (0-100) based on Sigil security scans:
- 90-100: LOW_RISK - Safe for production use
- 70-89: MEDIUM_RISK - Review findings before use
- 50-69: HIGH_RISK - Significant security concerns
- 0-49: CRITICAL_RISK - Do not use without thorough review

## Scan Phases
Sigil analyzes tools across 8 security phases:
1. Install Hooks (10x weight): Malicious install-time code
2. Code Patterns (5x weight): eval(), exec(), dangerous patterns
3. Network Exfiltration (3x weight): Outbound connections, webhooks
4. Credentials (2x weight): API keys, tokens, secrets
5. Obfuscation (5x weight): Hidden/encoded payloads
6. Provenance (1-3x weight): Binary files, suspicious origins
7. Prompt Injection: AI-specific attack patterns
8. Skill Security: Agent tool abuse patterns

## Example Usage

### Search for database tools:
GET https://api.sigilsec.ai/forge/search?q=postgres&type=both

### Get a stack for database work:
GET https://api.sigilsec.ai/forge/stack?use_case=query%20postgres%20database

### Check a specific tool:
GET https://api.sigilsec.ai/forge/tools/mcp/mcp-postgres

## Integration
Configure your AI agent to use Sigil Forge for tool discovery:

```json
{
  "mcp_servers": {
    "sigil": {
      "command": "sigil-mcp-server"
    }
  }
}
```

Then use forge_search and forge_stack tools to discover and evaluate tools before installation.

## Security First
Always run sigil_scan on tools before deployment. Trust scores are automated assessments, not security certifications. Review all HIGH/CRITICAL findings before production use.

## Support
- Documentation: https://sigilsec.ai/docs
- API Status: https://status.sigilsec.ai
- Security Reports: security@sigilsec.ai
- General Support: support@sigilsec.ai
